title: The Exact Value of R(B15, B15)
lean_gate_reason: Lean would only formalize a known theorem instance. The publication blocker is rediscovery, not missing formal sealing, so Lean is archival polish only and should not be used as the next step for this slug.
micro_paper_assessment: Fail the micro-paper lane. The Paley(61) witness is correct, but Wesley 2026 records the classical prime-power theorem implying R(B15,B15)=62, so this slug is a rediscovery rather than a frontier one-shot paper.
family_affinity: High affinity with diagonal book Ramsey numbers at the conference-graph threshold.
generalization_signal: Paley/conference constructions scale to q ≡ 1 mod 4 prime powers, but exactness does not automatically propagate to neighboring n.
strongest_honest_claim: The Paley coloring of K_61 has monochromatic book number exactly 14 in both colors, but this only rederives the already-known exact value R(B15,B15)=62 covered by the prime-power diagonal book theorem recorded in Wesley 2026.
paper_title_hint: No new-paper title recommended; internal reference only: Paley(61) Witness for the Known Value R(B15,B15)=62.
candidate_theorem_slice: The Paley coloring of K_61 realizes the known lower-bound slice R(B15,B15) >= 62 inside the classical prime-power diagonal book theorem.
